message:
fix(guard): separate archive root from repo root, fix pattern overlap and push path detection

The guard_check and guard_check_full APIs now accept separate archive_root and
repo_root parameters. Previously, a single path was used for both reading
reservation archives and detecting git config (core.ignorecase). This caused
incorrect case-sensitivity detection when the archive root differed from the
working repository. All call sites and tests are updated accordingly.

The pre-push hook's get_push_paths now correctly handles new-branch pushes by
using `git rev-list <sha> --not --remotes` instead of `git rev-list <sha>`
alone. The old behavior would enumerate ALL reachable commits for a new branch,
potentially including thousands of irrelevant commits. The Python guard plugin
script receives the same fix for parity. The fallback diff path is now only
attempted when a range is available (not for new branches where it would fail).

Non-glob reservation patterns (literal directory names like "src/utils") now
correctly match contained files (e.g. "src/utils/file.rs") via a new reverse
prefix check in check_path_conflicts.

pattern_overlap: segments_overlap now returns true on length mismatch (one path
is a directory prefix of the other), and the heuristic check is applied to all
pattern combinations, not just glob-vs-glob. This fixes false negatives where
exact paths nested inside reserved directories were not detected as conflicts.

config.rs: unescape_double_quotes now preserves the backslash for unrecognized
escape sequences (e.g. \a becomes \a instead of just a), matching standard
string escaping behavior.

storage: removed redundant pre-read size check in store_raw_attachment (the
read itself handles size limits), and applied formatting cleanup to
coalescer_pool_worker's fetch_update call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
